kai_chong.name: Kai Chong
kai_chong.summary:
Hailing from Massachusetts, Kai Chong has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U17 BD, U17 XD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 8 career titles across 16 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Notably, Kai has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Kai especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, 4 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U17 BD, U17 XD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals top-3 (BD U17, XD U17) • 7 ORC titles.

Kai has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 10 different players across the career:
  • Jeffrey Chang (current primary partner) — 8 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (8 total events in BD/U19, across 2 seasons)
  • Stella Pan (current primary partner) — 7 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (7 total events in U19/XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Matthew Li — 1 events, 1 title together in BD

Kai has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Kai has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 13 seeded entries across the career, Kai consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 4 seasons and 8 titles, Kai has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

Out of Illinois, Ketan Dubey is a Regional Championship titlist, currently competing at the U13 level, proving the ability to triumph against the strongest regional fields. Open Regional Championships draw the top talent from across the region, and taking home the title is a genuine achievement.

Notably, Ketan has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Ketan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: 2 ORC titles.

Ketan has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 6 different players across the career:
  • Melvin Cui (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (4 total events in BD, across 2 seasons)
  • Janice Chen (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (3 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Kennis He (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, best: #2)

The 2022-2023 season tells a story of acceleration, with 4 titles across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Ketan is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Ketan has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Ketan isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Ketan consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Ketan's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

kevin_xie.name: Kevin Xie
kevin_xie.summary:
Out of Maryland, Kevin Xie wears the gold from Junior Nationals — the single most important tournament for any American junior player in BD U11. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 10 career victories across 23 tournaments, Kevin has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Kevin is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Kevin is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 3 singles titles, 5 doubles titles, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (3 entries) • Junior Nationals champion (BD U11) • 5 ORC titles.

Diving into the match data reveals: Kevin boasts 62 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a biggest win margin of 33 points, a commanding 76% win rate across 99 matches.

With 11 different doubles partners over the years, Kevin adapts to anyone:
  • Anirudh Rudharraju (current primary partner) — an ongoing partnership that keeps producing results: 4 titles across 17 events in BD, spanning 4 seasons
  • Claire QiuChen Li (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in U13/XD (9 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Alaina Benjamin (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD, across 2 seasons
  • Zelenia Shen — 5 events, 1 title together in XD, across 2 seasons

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 8 podium finishes across 5 tournaments. Kevin is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Kevin has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Kevin has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Kevin has been seeded #1 or #2 in 10 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 10 titles, Kevin is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Representing California, Kyle Wang has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U19 BD, U17 BD, U17 XD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 10 career titles across 13 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Kyle is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, 6 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U19 BD, U17 BD, U17 XD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (XD U19, BD U19) • 6 ORC titles.

Kyle has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 8 different players across the career:
  • Ella Yishan Lin (current primary partner) — an ongoing partnership that keeps producing results: 3 titles across 7 events in U17/U19/XD, spanning 3 seasons
  • Ryan Ma (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (4 total events in BD/U19, across 2 seasons)
  • Weslie Chen (current primary partner) — 3 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (3 total events in BD, across 2 seasons)
  • Audrey Chang (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, best: #2)

The 2023-2024 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 3 tournaments. Kyle is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Kyle has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Kyle has competed across 4 different regions (NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 7 seeded entries across the career, Kyle consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 10 titles, Kyle is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

kyle_zhan.name: Kyle Zhan
kyle_zhan.summary:
Representing California, Kyle Zhan is steadily building a competitive resume, currently competing at the U19 level, with 9 entries across 5 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

Notably, Kyle has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Kyle competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

Kyle has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning NE, NorCal, SoCal, Kyle isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Kyle brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

kyler_trice.name: Kyler Trice
kyler_trice.summary:
Representing Texas, Kyler Trice has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U15 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 31 entries in 13 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

What makes Kyler especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

Kyler has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 11 different players across the career:
  • Samiksh Talapally (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (3 events, best: #3)
  • Vedant Shukla (current partner) — currently teaming up in BD (2 events)
  • Chingpei Lin (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events)

Kyler has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Kyler has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Kyler brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

kylie_ou.name: Kylie Ou
kylie_ou.summary:
Representing California, Kylie Ou has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-3), currently competing at the U11 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Kylie's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

A true triple-threat, Kylie competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, a doubles best of #3, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: ORC top-3 finisher.

With 5 different doubles partners over the years, Kylie adapts to anyone:
  • Bowen Wang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Adalyn Puah (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, best: #3)

Kylie is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Kylie is still ascending.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Kylie brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.
